source.title: NVO & LLY Quarterly Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, and Capital Allocation — Yahoo Finance API (Q4 2024 – Q4 2025)

extractions.claim: NVO net debt expanded to DKK 104.0B (Q4 2025) from DKK 76.5B (Q4 2024), a 36% increase in 12 months
extractions.evidence: Q4 2024: cash+ST investments DKK 26.3B, total debt DKK 102.8B, net debt DKK 76.5B. Q4 2025: cash+ST investments DKK 27.0B, total debt DKK 131.0B, net debt DKK 104.0B. Debt rose DKK 28.2B while liquid assets flat.

verification.verifier_notes: Computed from Yahoo Finance fundamentalsTimeSeries quarterly data

extractions.claim: NVO Q4 2025 FCF collapsed to negative DKK 36.2B, driven by DKK 43.8B capex (2.7x the prior quarter average of DKK 15.7B)
extractions.evidence: Q4 2025: operatingCashFlow DKK 7.6B, capitalExpenditure DKK -43.8B, freeCashFlow DKK -36.2B. Prior 3 quarters (Q1-Q3 2025) capex averaged DKK 15.4B. Q4 capex was 2.85x Q1-Q3 average.

verification.verifier_notes: Capex spike likely includes Catalent acquisition close and manufacturing expansion. Q4 OpCF also depressed (DKK 7.6B vs Q3's 46.1B).

extractions.claim: NVO TTM operating cash flow was DKK 119.1B (FY2025), but TTM FCF was only DKK 29.0B due to DKK 90.1B in total capex
extractions.evidence: FY2025 quarterly OpCF: 24.6 + 40.8 + 46.1 + 7.6 = DKK 119.1B. FY2025 quarterly capex: 14.6 + 16.3 + 15.4 + 43.8 = DKK 90.1B. FCF = 119.1 - 90.1 = DKK 29.0B (matches sum of quarterly FCF: 10.0 + 24.5 + 30.7 - 36.2 = DKK 29.0B).

verification.verifier_notes: Yahoo reports TTM FCF at DKK 0.4B via stats endpoint — discrepancy may be due to Yahoo's trailing calculation methodology vs calendar year sum

extractions.claim: NVO financed its capex surge with DKK 103.9B in gross debt issuance during FY2025, while repaying only DKK 79.2B
extractions.evidence: FY2025 issuanceOfDebt: 27.4 + 45.2 + 0.7 + 30.6 = DKK 103.9B. repaymentOfDebt: 12.0 + 64.9 + 1.1 + 1.2 = DKK 79.2B. Net new borrowing: DKK 24.7B.

verification.verifier_notes: Consistent with total debt increase from DKK 102.8B to DKK 131.0B (+28.2B, close to net issuance of 24.7B after rounding/FX)

extractions.claim: NVO's long-term debt grew from DKK 84.2B to DKK 111.7B (+33%) while short-term debt stayed roughly flat (DKK 11.8B to DKK 10.7B)
extractions.evidence: Q4 2024: LT debt DKK 84.2B, current debt DKK 11.8B. Q4 2025: LT debt DKK 111.7B, current debt DKK 10.7B. The company termed out borrowings.

verification.verifier_notes: Yahoo Finance quarterly balance sheet data

extractions.claim: NVO has persistent negative working capital (DKK -43.2B in Q4 2025), with accounts payable of DKK 153.1B dwarfing receivables of DKK 70.9B
extractions.evidence: Q4 2025: currentAssets DKK 172.5B, currentLiabilities DKK 215.7B, workingCapital DKK -43.2B. Accounts payable DKK 153.1B vs accounts receivable DKK 70.9B. Inventory DKK 49.6B.

verification.verifier_notes: AP spike in Q4 may include accrued Catalent-related liabilities or year-end timing

extractions.claim: NVO stockholders' equity grew 35% to DKK 194.0B (Q4 2025), driven by retained earnings of DKK 195.3B, giving debt/equity of 67.5%
extractions.evidence: Q4 2024: equity DKK 143.5B, retained earnings DKK 144.4B. Q4 2025: equity DKK 194.0B, retained earnings DKK 195.3B. Debt/equity 67.5% per Yahoo stats.

verification.verifier_notes: FY2025 net income was ~DKK 102.4B, supporting the ~DKK 50.9B increase in retained earnings after dividends/buybacks

extractions.claim: NVO interest expense was DKK 10.8B in FY2025 (H1 only: Q1 DKK 5.2B + Q2 DKK 5.0B), while interest income was DKK 13.0B, resulting in net interest income
extractions.evidence: Interest expense: Q4 2024 DKK 5.1B, Q1 2025 DKK 5.2B, Q2 2025 DKK 5.0B, Q3 missing, Q4 2025 DKK -4.4B (likely data anomaly). Interest income: Q4 2024 DKK 3.9B, Q1 DKK 3.4B, Q2 DKK 5.3B, Q3 DKK 1.8B, Q4 DKK 2.4B (FY2025 total: DKK 13.0B).

verification.verifier_notes: Q3 and Q4 interest expense data incomplete/anomalous from Yahoo. Interest coverage still comfortable given DKK 128B operating income.

extractions.claim: LLY net debt was $35.2B (Q4 2025): $7.3B cash vs $42.5B total debt — a higher leverage ratio with debt/equity of 165.3%
extractions.evidence: Q4 2025: cash DKK 7.3B, total debt $42.5B (LT $40.9B, current $1.6B). Stockholders' equity $26.5B. Debt/equity 165.3% per Yahoo stats.

verification.verifier_notes: LLY debt/equity much higher than NVO's 67.5% due to lower equity base relative to debt

extractions.claim: LLY TTM FCF was $6.4B (FY2025) on $16.8B OpCF minus $10.8B capex — LLY's capex run-rate is also elevated for manufacturing
extractions.evidence: FY2025 quarterly OpCF: 1.7 + 3.1 + 8.8 + 3.2 = $16.8B. Capex: 3.3 + 1.8 + 2.8 + 3.0 = $10.8B (est). FCF: -1.6 + 1.3 + 6.0 + 0.3 = $6.0B. Yahoo stats report TTM FCF at $1.95B.

verification.verifier_notes: Yahoo FCF stat may use different quarter boundaries. Sum of quarterly data is more reliable.

extractions.claim: LLY returned $10.3B to shareholders in FY2025: $4.1B buybacks + $5.4B dividends (est), absorbing 61% of TTM OpCF
extractions.evidence: FY2025 buybacks: 1.2 + 0.7 + 0.7 + 1.5 = $4.1B. Dividends: 1.3 + 1.3 + 1.3 + 1.3 = $5.4B (est). Total: $9.5B. OpCF was $16.8B. Capital return / OpCF = 57%.

verification.verifier_notes: Conservative ratio. LLY also increasing debt to fund capex + returns.

extractions.claim: NVO capex surged in Q4 2025 to DKK 43.8B — likely reflecting the Catalent acquisition close (~$16.5B / ~DKK 115B) partially hitting capex/investing lines
extractions.evidence: Q4 2025 investingCashFlow was DKK -42.8B and capex DKK -43.8B. Q4 2025 also saw DKK 30.6B in debt issuance (financingCashFlow DKK +29.5B). Catalent acquisition announced Dec 2023, closed Feb 2025 per public filings.

verification.verifier_notes: Catalent likely closed Q1 2025 not Q4. The Q4 2025 capex spike may be organic manufacturing buildout (fill-finish plants in Denmark, US, France). Need to verify against 20-F.

extractions.claim: NVO inventory grew steadily from DKK 40.8B to DKK 49.6B (+22%) over 5 quarters, consistent with GLP-1 production ramp
extractions.evidence: Inventory: Q4 2024 DKK 40.8B → Q1 2025 DKK 42.9B → Q2 DKK 45.7B → Q3 DKK 47.5B → Q4 DKK 49.6B. +21.5% in 12 months.

verification.verifier_notes: Consistent with NVO management guidance on building safety stock for semaglutide

extractions.claim: LLY inventory grew even faster: from $7.6B to $13.7B (+81%) over 5 quarters, reflecting tirzepatide manufacturing ramp

verification.verifier_notes: LLY has been aggressively building tirzepatide inventory to meet demand

extractions.claim: NVO can fund operations from OpCF alone ($119B DKK) but CANNOT self-fund capex ($90B) + shareholder returns, requiring ongoing debt issuance
extractions.evidence: FY2025 OpCF DKK 119.1B. Capex DKK 90.1B. Buybacks (partial data) DKK 8.9B minimum. Dividends not captured but substantial. Total uses exceed OpCF, hence DKK 24.7B net new debt.

verification.verifier_notes: This is the key finding: NVO's massive manufacturing buildout forces debt-funded capex despite strong operating profitability

extractions.claim: NVO stock price at $36.84 implies market cap ~$164B (ADR shares ~4.44B * $36.84), while enterprise value is $266B including DKK 104B net debt
extractions.evidence: Alpaca quote: NVO $36.835. Yahoo stats: EV $266.3B, shares outstanding 3.37B (B-shares basis). ADR = 1 B-share. Total shares including A+B = 4.44B.

verification.verifier_notes: Market cap calculation depends on share class treatment. Yahoo EV of $266B is the authoritative figure.

extractions.claim: LLY stock price at $910.42 implies market cap ~$813B (895M shares * $910), with EV $847B — 3.2x NVO's EV despite only 4.2x higher revenue in USD terms
extractions.evidence: Alpaca quote: LLY $910.42. Yahoo stats: EV $846.7B, shares outstanding 893M. NVO EV $266B. LLY revenue $65.2B vs NVO $309B DKK (~$42B USD at 7.3 DKK/USD). LLY/NVO revenue ratio ~1.55x, but EV ratio 3.18x.

verification.verifier_notes: LLY commands significant premium. DKK/USD rate assumed ~7.3 for comparison.
